description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_enumeration, mutagen.mutators

Deliverable:
1. New module `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/dispatch.ex` exposing `MutagenEx.Mutators.Dispatch.mutators_for_node/1`. The module carries a static mapping head_atom => mutator list AND a list of `:any` mutators (Literal, ResultTuple, etc. — mutators that match bare literals which are NOT 3-tuples). Per mutagen.decision.static_mutator_dispatch, this is a pure module — NO behaviour callback.
2. `mutators_for_node/1` returns a sub-sequence of `MutagenEx.Mutators.all/0`, filtered in canonical order. This is the order-preserving property required for byte-identity (mutagen.r2 determinism).
3. `MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator.try_mutators/6` consults `Dispatch.mutators_for_node/1` to pre-filter the mutator list before calling `match?/1` on each. Add a `:dispatch_mode` option to `enumerate/4` (default `:head_atom`, override `:legacy` for the equivalence test ONLY).
4. New `test/mutagen_ex/head_atom_dispatch_test.exs` with the equivalence test: for a corpus of AST nodes, assert `Mutators.all/0` (legacy path) and `Dispatch.mutators_for_node/1` (new path), applied via `match?/1` filtering, produce identical mutator lists for each node.
5. Revise `.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md` to document the dispatch optimization (likely a new requirement covering the order-preserving + equivalence properties).
6. NO mutator behaviour change. `mutators.ex` is NOT touched.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test` green; new `head_atom_dispatch_test.exs` green (equivalence holds for the corpus); existing `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs` green
- Behaviors to demonstrate: determinism test green (the byte-identity check would catch any reordering bug)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/*.ex EXCEPT the new dispatch.ex (do not touch existing mutator implementations)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex (the behaviour module — DO NOT add a `head_atoms/0` callback)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (S4/S5 own)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex (S2 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/beam_cache.ex (S5 owns)
Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/dispatch.ex (new)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (try_mutators/6 + :dispatch_mode opt only)
- test/mutagen_ex/head_atom_dispatch_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT add `@callback head_atoms/0` or `@optional_callbacks head_atoms: 0` to mutators.ex. This is a HARD constraint per mutagen.decision.static_mutator_dispatch. The auditor will check.
- Do NOT touch any mutator module (literal.ex, arith.ex, case_drop.ex, etc.). The dispatch table lives in one file.
- Do NOT reorder Mutators.all/0. The dispatch table FILTERS, never reorders.
- The `:dispatch_mode` option is a test seam only — it is NOT documented in the public API and is NOT exposed via the Mix task.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- [ ] `mix test` green
- [ ] Determinism test green (catches reordering regressions)
- [ ] Head-atom equivalence test green
- [ ] Mutator behaviour callback list unchanged (grep verification: `@callback` + `@optional_callbacks` lines in mutators.ex unchanged)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: `mutagen-wrd.25.03: head-atom dispatch table + enumerator pre-filter`
title: S3: head-atom dispatch table + enumerator pre-filter
